title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.imageCaption: Serve this show-stopping whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi for an unforgettable Middle Eastern feast!

description: Experience the grandeur of Middle Eastern cuisine with Lamb Ouzi, a spectacular whole slow roasted lamb dish. This celebratory meal features tender, aromatic lamb, often stuffed with spiced rice and nuts, cooked to perfection. It's a centerpiece that promises an unforgettable culinary journey for any special occasion.

instructions.title: Prepare and Marinate the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb dry. In a small bowl, combine olive oil, minced garlic, seven spice, cinnamon, cardamom, salt, and black pepper to create a paste. Rub this mixture generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into any crevices. For best results, cover and refrigerate overnight, or for at least 4 hours.

instructions.title: Prepare the Aromatic Rice Stuffing
instructions.text: In a large pot, heat a tablespoon of olive oil. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes. If using ground meat, add it now and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon. Drain any excess fat. Add the soaked and drained basmati rice, 1/4 cup of pine nuts, 1/4 cup of almonds, 1 teaspoon of seven spice, 1/2 teaspoon of cinnamon, and salt and pepper to taste. Stir well to combine. Add 3 cups of broth and 1 cup of water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until rice is partially cooked.

instructions.title: Stuff and Prepare for Roasting
instructions.text: Carefully stuff the partially cooked rice mixture into the cavity of the lamb shoulder or leg. If the cavity is small, you can also place some rice around the lamb in the roasting pan. Secure the opening with kitchen twine or skewers to keep the stuffing inside. Place the stuffed lamb in a large, sturdy roasting pan.

instructions.title: Initial Roasting and Sealing
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Pour the remaining 2 cups of broth and 1 cup of water into the bottom of the roasting pan. Cover the roasting pan tightly with aluminum foil, ensuring a good seal to trap moisture. Roast for 2 hours at 180°C (350°F).

instructions.title: Slow Roast to Tenderness
instructions.text: Reduce the oven temperature to 150°C (300°F). Continue roasting the lamb, still covered, for an additional 3 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls away from the bone. Baste the lamb with the pan juices every hour. The total cooking time will vary depending on the size of the lamb.

instructions.title: Crisp and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the foil for the last 30-45 minutes of cooking to allow the lamb skin to crisp up and brown. Once cooked, carefully remove the lamb from the oven and transfer it to a large serving platter. Loosely tent with foil and let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes before carving. Garnish generously with the remaining toasted pine nuts, almonds, fresh parsley, and mint. Serve the succulent lamb with the flavorful rice stuffing and any accumulated pan juices.

tips: For a deeply flavorful lamb, marinate it overnight in the refrigerator. This allows the spices to penetrate fully and results in a more aromatic dish.

If you cannot find a whole lamb shoulder or leg, large bone-in lamb shanks or a boneless lamb roast can be used. Adjust cooking times accordingly, but maintain the slow roasting method for tenderness.
Always rest the roasted lamb for at least 20-30 minutes before carving. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a more tender and moist result.
article:
Lamb Ouzi: The Ultimate Whole Slow Roasted Middle Eastern Feast

Imagine a dish that embodies the warmth, hospitality, and rich culinary heritage of the Middle East. A dish so magnificent it graces tables at weddings, Eid celebrations, and grand family gatherings. That dish is Lamb Ouzi, a masterpiece of slow-roasted lamb, aromatic spiced rice, and toasted nuts. Far more than just a meal, it’s an experience, a symphony of flavors and textures that tells a story of tradition and love. If you're looking to impress with an authentic Middle Eastern dinner recipe that promises fall-off-the-bone tenderness and exquisite aroma, mastering the Lamb Ouzi recipe is your next culinary adventure.

## What is Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, sometimes spelled "Ozzie" or "Ozi," is a beloved Middle Eastern dish renowned for its celebratory presence. At its heart, it features a generous cut of lamb – often a whole leg or shoulder – that has been slow-roasted to absolute perfection, rendering it incredibly tender and succulent. This magnificent lamb is typically served atop a bed of fragrant basmati rice, richly spiced with a blend of warming Middle Eastern spices like cinnamon, cardamom, and allspice. Toasted nuts, such as pine nuts, almonds, or pistachios, are sprinkled generously over the top, adding a delightful crunch and nutty depth.

While there are simpler versions of Ouzi that involve ground lamb mixed into the rice, the true spectacle, and the focus of this guide, is the "whole slow roasted" method. This technique elevates the dish, allowing the lamb to develop an unparalleled depth of flavor and a melt-in-your-mouth texture that defines this iconic Middle Eastern dinner recipe. It's a dish that demands time and patience, but the reward is an unforgettable feast that will transport your taste buds straight to the heart of the Levant. Understanding the nuances of the Lamb Ouzi recipe is key to unlocking its full potential.

## Ingredients for Lamb Ouzi

Crafting the perfect Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi begins with selecting quality ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need:

For the Lamb:
*   1 whole bone-in lamb leg or shoulder (4-6 lbs / 2-3 kg)
*   1/4 cup olive oil
*   6-8 cloves garlic, minced
*   Juice of 1 lemon
*   2 tsp salt
*   1 tsp black pepper
*   1 tsp ground cumin
*   1 tsp ground coriander
*   1 tsp paprika
*   1/2 tsp ground turmeric
*   1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
*   1/2 tsp ground cardamom
*   1/4 tsp ground allspice
*   Optional: 1-2 cups chicken or lamb broth for roasting

For the Ouzi Rice:
*   3 cups basmati rice, rinsed until water runs clear
*   2 tbsp olive oil or ghee
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   3 cloves garlic, minced
*   1 tsp salt
*   1/2 tsp black pepper
*   1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
*   1/4 tsp ground cardamom
*   1/4 tsp ground allspice
*   4 cups chicken or lamb broth
*   1 cup frozen green peas (optional)

For the Garnish:
*   1/2 cup pine nuts, toasted
*   1/2 cup slivered almonds, toasted
*   Fresh parsley, chopped

## How to Make Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

The magic of Lamb Ouzi lies in its slow preparation, allowing flavors to meld and the lamb to become incredibly tender. Here's a step-by-step guide to this magnificent Middle Eastern dinner recipe:

1.  **Prepare the Lamb:** Pat the lamb leg or shoulder dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, score the fatty side of the lamb in a diamond pattern, about 1/2 inch deep. In a small bowl, combine olive oil, minced garlic, lemon juice, salt, pepper, cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, cinnamon, cardamom, and allspice. Rub this spice mixture generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the scored crevices. For best results, cover the lamb and refrigerate to marinate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight.

2.  **Slow Roast the Lamb:** Preheat your oven to 300°F (150°C). Place the marinated lamb in a large, heavy-bottomed roasting pan or Dutch oven. Add 1-2 cups of chicken or lamb broth to the bottom of the pan – this will create a moist environment and prevent drying. Cover the pan tightly with foil or a lid. Roast for 4-6 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls away from the bone. The exact time will depend on the size of your lamb cut. For the last 30-45 minutes, remove the foil/lid to allow the lamb to brown and develop a beautiful crust. Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven, transfer it to a cutting board, cover loosely with foil, and let it rest for at least 20-30 minutes before carving or shredding. This resting period is crucial for juicy lamb.

3.  **Prepare the Ouzi Rice:** While the lamb is resting, begin preparing the rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil or ghee over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the rinsed basmati rice, salt, pepper, cinnamon, cardamom, and allspice. Toast the rice and spices for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the rice grains appear slightly translucent at the edges.

4.  **Cook the Rice:** Pour in the chicken or lamb broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then immediately reduce the heat to low, cover the pot tightly, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender. Do not lift the lid during this time. Once cooked, turn off the heat and let the rice stand, covered, for another 10 minutes. If using, gently stir in the frozen green peas at this stage, allowing the residual heat to warm them through. Fluff the rice with a fork.

5.  **Toast the Nuts & Assemble:** In a dry pan over medium heat, toast the pine nuts and slivered almonds separately until golden brown and fragrant. Be careful, as they can burn quickly.

6.  **The complete Lamb Ouzi recipe involves a grand presentation.** To assemble, spread the fragrant Ouzi rice onto a large serving platter. Carefully place the whole slow-roasted lamb (either whole or artfully shredded/carved) on top of the rice. Garnish generously with the toasted pine nuts, slivered almonds, and fresh chopped parsley.

## Tips for the Best Lamb Ouzi

*   **Quality Lamb:** Opt for a good quality bone-in lamb leg or shoulder. The bone adds incredible flavor during the slow roasting process.
*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skip the marination step, especially overnight. This allows the spices to deeply penetrate the meat, resulting in maximum flavor.
*   **Low and Slow:** The secret to fall-off-the-bone tender lamb is a low oven temperature and a long cooking time. Patience is rewarded!
*   **Cover Tightly:** Keeping the lamb tightly covered for most of the cooking ensures moisture retention and tenderness.
*   **Rest the Lamb:** Always rest the lamb after roasting. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a more succulent final product.
*   **Rinse Rice Thoroughly:** Rinsing the basmati rice until the water runs clear removes excess starch, resulting in fluffier, separate grains.
*   **Fresh Spices:** Use fresh, good-quality spices for the most aromatic and flavorful dish.
*   **Toast Nuts:** Toasting the nuts right before serving adds a fresh, irresistible crunch and enhances their flavor.

## Serving Suggestions for Ouzi

This Lamb Ouzi recipe is a celebration on a platter, designed to be the centerpiece of any festive meal. Serve it traditionally on a large, communal platter, allowing guests to help themselves. It pairs beautifully with a side of cooling plain yogurt or a refreshing cucumber-yogurt sauce (laban). A vibrant Middle Eastern salad like Fattoush or Tabbouleh provides a lovely counterpoint to the richness of the lamb and rice. Pickled vegetables and warm pita bread are also excellent accompaniments.

## Variations & Substitutions

While this article focuses on the classic whole slow roasted Lamb Ouzi recipe, there are ways to adapt it:

*   **Meat:** While lamb is traditional, you could experiment with a bone-in goat leg or a large beef roast, adjusting cooking times as needed.
*   **Spices:** Feel free to adjust the spice blend to your preference. A pinch of dried rose petals can add a unique floral note, or a touch of cayenne pepper for heat.
*   **Vegetables:** Add chunks of carrots, potatoes, or bell peppers to the roasting pan during the last hour for extra flavor and a complete meal.
*   **Rice:** For a healthier twist, brown basmati rice can be used, though it will require a longer cooking time and more liquid.
*   **Quicker Ouzi:** For a weeknight version, you can adapt the rice recipe by cooking ground lamb with the spices and mixing it directly into the rice, foregoing the whole slow-roasted lamb (though the grandeur will be diminished!).

Lamb Ouzi is more than just a Middle Eastern dinner recipe; it's an experience, a labor of love that results in an incredibly satisfying and memorable meal. The tender, aromatic lamb, the perfectly spiced rice, and the crunchy nuts create a symphony of flavors and textures that will undoubtedly impress your guests and warm your home. Don't hesitate to try this incredible Lamb Ouzi recipe; the journey from preparation to plate is as rewarding as the first delicious bite.
